input:
One day, an idiot stopped the Prophet and threw dust over his head. Some of the Quraysh leaders were delighted to see the Prophet being publicly humiliated. He went home with dust on his head. One of his daughters went up to him to clean his head, tears pouring down her cheeks. He comforted her saying: “Do not cry, young daughter, God will protect your father.” In an authentic ḥadīth, Muslim relates on the authority of ʿAbdullāh ibn Masʿūd: God’s Messenger was once praying at the Kaʿbah, while Abū Jahl sat with a few of his friends not far away.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 12: Difficulties in Abundance

output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 12: Difficulties in Abundance

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown without face, Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: Abū Jahl ibn Hishām
characters.appearance: Late 40s, dark olive skin, muscular build, strong features. Thick beard, neatly kept. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, dark brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Frowning, scowling eyes, aggressive posture.

characters.name: Zaynab bint Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Late teens, light olive skin, slender build, gentle features. Wearing a simple white thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, light-coloured cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Tears streaming down her cheeks, looking up at her father with concern.

output.moral_lesson: Patience and forgiveness are key to dealing with difficult situations and people.
output.narrative_text: One day, a man from the Quraysh tribe teased the Prophet by throwing dust on his head. The Prophet was humiliated in front of many people. His daughter, Zaynab, saw her father's condition and cried. The Prophet comforted her saying, ‘Do not cry, young daughter, God will protect your father’.

compliance.notes: The Prophet is shown without face, in accordance with Islamic depiction rules. Abu Jahl is shown as an antagonist, while the Prophet's daughter is shown with tears, a gentle and emotional scene, suitable for children.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. Exterior of a 7th century Makkah mud-brick home. A man in white thobe and green cloak, white turban, seen from the back, standing with dust on his head. Outside, a group of men, including Abu Jahl, are shown in the distance, laughing and teasing. A young girl, wearing white thobe and brown cloak, is shown running towards her father, tears streaming down her face. Sunlight in the background. Medium shot, eye level. Warm golden palette. No modern objects.
